chemistry.reaction_name: Photoredox-catalyzed fragmentation and cyanation of catharanthine

mechanistic_details.key_intermediate: radical cation 13, ring opened radical cation 14

chemistry.scope_summary: Method applied to synthesis of multiple alkaloids including (-)-pseudotabersonine, (-)-pseudovincadifformine, and (+)-coronaridine from catharanthine. Flow conditions improved efficiency with 2 min residence time and 96% yield.

figures.caption: Scheme 1. Catharanthine Fragmentation Provides Access to Structurally Diverse Alkaloids

key_observations_supporting_mechanism: Batch: 93% yield, 3 h; Flow: 96% yield, tR = 2 min for catharanthine fragmentation
13% yield for pseudotabersonine 2, 14% yield for compound 7, 6% yield for compound 8 in acid-mediated rearrangement
65% yield for pseudotabersonine 2 from mixture of compounds 7 and 8 with TFA
90% yield at reflux 3 h with 2:1 e.r., 5-24% yield at 60°C 6 h with 20:1 e.r.
figures.caption: Scheme 2. Synthesis of ( − )-Pseudotabersonine | Scheme 3. Synthesis of (+)-Coronaridine
Scheme 2. Synthesis of ( − )-Pseudotabersonine | Scheme 3. Synthesis of (+)-Coronaridine

key_observations_supporting_mechanism: ring strain values of -4.23 kcal/mol, +0.08 kcal/mol, and -0.00 kcal/mol for different transformations
piperidine conformation depends upon ethyl configuration

key_observations_supporting_mechanism: 96% yield for visible-light promoted fragmentation step
Formation of multiple natural products from common intermediate 6
figures.caption: Scheme 4. Synthesis of ( − )-Pseudovincadi ff ormine | Scheme 5. Proposed Catalytic Cycle for Fragmentation | Scheme 6. Photocatalytic Fragmentation of Hydrogenated Iboga  Alkaloids | Scheme 7. Homodesmotic Strain Release Values | Scheme 8. Synthetic summary

translation_logic.batch_limitation: Longer reaction time required (3 hours) with slightly lower yield (93%)
translation_logic.flow_advantage: Dramatically reduced reaction time (2 minutes) with higher yield (96%)
translation_logic.what_changed: Reaction time reduced from 3 hours to 2 minutes residence time
translation_logic.what_stayed_same: Temperature (60°C), solvent (MeOH), catalyst system, and overall reaction conditions
translation_logic.safety_improvement: Continuous flow processing reduces solvent inventory and provides better heat management for photochemical reactions

figure_descriptions: Multiple mechanistic schemes showing photoredox catalytic cycle and alkaloid synthesis pathways
Strain analysis calculations for ring systems
Synthetic summary showing flow fragmentation step

notes: Key fragmentation step data clearly presented in figures. Flow conditions show dramatic time reduction from 180 min to 2 min with yield improvement from 93% to 96%. Reactor volume given as 1.34 mL. No optimization table present but mechanistic schemes provide detailed reaction pathway information.
